description: Clean-slate SEC filing agentic review compiler executed by Ralph one story at a time and administered by Claude. The build is governed by the no-fallback, no-backward-compatibility, no-overengineering, no-overfit, no-patchlike-behavior, always-first-principle doctrine.

userStories.title: Freeze Linkflow GO evidence and Ralph governance
userStories.description: As Claude administering Ralph, I need the specs and handoff to name the committed Linkflow probe run and Ralph execution model before any code is added.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-agentic-review-compiler-design.md states that execution is Claude-administered Ralph, not /goal

docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-agentic-review-compiler-design.md states the no-fallback, no-backward-compatibility, no-overengineering, no-overfit, no-patchlike-behavior, always-first-principle doctrine
docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-08-agentic-review-compiler-ralph-implementation.md is the active human-readable implementation plan
ralph/prd.json is the active Ralph story list
python3 -m linkflow_probe summarize runs/linkflow-probe/20260508T123815Z prints Gate: GO
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Create package skeleton and run kernel
userStories.description: As the compiler runtime, I need a new sec_review_compiler package with deterministic run ids, manifests, atomic artifact writes, and a minimal CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: src/sec_review_compiler package exists with __init__.py, __main__.py, cli.py, config.py, errors.py, and run modules
Run ids validate the shape YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ_<slug>_<8hex>
RunManifest includes run_id, started_at, config_hash, linkflow_probe_run_id, and package_version
Atomic JSON/text writes use temp file plus replace
python3 -m sec_review_compiler init-run --deal-slug synthetic-demo --run-root runs/dev creates a run manifest
tests/test_run_kernel.py covers invalid ids, manifest fields, atomic writes, and CLI init
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Build filing package and atlas
userStories.description: As the evidence layer, I need immutable filing packages, exact source spans, and atlas records before any agent can propose facts.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/synthetic_filing.txt contains a transaction heading, background section, dated Buyer A paragraphs, a table-like block, and an exhibit marker
FilingPackage stores filing_id, raw_text, raw_sha256, normalized_text, and ordered paragraphs
Paragraph splitting preserves source order and original character offsets
SourceSpan.identity() hashes filing_id, char_start, char_end, and quote_text_hash
Atlas records sections, paragraphs, tables, source_spans, section_candidates, and atlas_warnings
Ambiguous section labels create atlas_warnings instead of silent skips
Missing substantive tender-offer exhibit raises a typed error
tests/test_filing_atlas.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Add retrieval index and deterministic tools
userStories.description: As an agent support layer, I need deterministic local tools for search, quote verification, parsing, and filing-local actor labels.
acceptanceCriteria: RetrievalIndex supports literal lookup, regex lookup, BM25-style token scoring, section fetch, paragraph fetch, neighborhood fetch, and table fetch
verify_quote returns verbatim_present, positions, paragraph_ids, and ambiguity
parse_date, parse_money, and parse_count return conservative structured values
parse_date('early March 2026') returns an ambiguous structured value rather than a fake exact date
parse_money('$14.00 per share') preserves per-share unit semantics
parse_count('between 20 and 25 parties') returns min 20 and max 25
normalize_actor_label is filing-local only and does not create cross-deal actor pooling
tests/test_retrieval_tools.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Create deal-room DuckDB schema and lifecycle
userStories.description: As the deal-room authority, I need append-oriented tables for claims, evidence, verifier verdicts, coverage, conflicts, human decisions, and canonical rows.
acceptanceCriteria: DuckDB schema defines source_records, claim_attempts, evidence_bindings, normalized_values, verifier_verdicts, verdict_aggregates, coverage_checks, conflicts, human_decisions, canonical_rows, and canonical_row_evidence or documented equivalent normalized tables
Lifecycle states include proposed, binding_failed, bound, verified_confirmed, verified_partial, verified_rejected, escalated, consistent, accepted, and superseded
Partial corrections create new attempt_id values and never mutate original attempts
attempt_id and claim_fingerprint are distinct concepts covered by tests
Duplicate semantic facts from two agents remain separate attempts
Latest-verdict-wins behavior is impossible under lifecycle tests
failed_to_check coverage blocks trusted publication
tests/test_deal_room_schema.py and tests/test_lifecycle.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Implement Linkflow adapter and tool loop
userStories.description: As the model boundary, I need Linkflow direct SDK calls, strict output schemas, environment-only credentials, and an explicit Python tool loop.
acceptanceCriteria: LinkflowClientConfig reads LINKFLOW_API_KEY, LINKFLOW_BASE_URL, LINKFLOW_MODEL, LINKFLOW_REASONING_EFFORT, and concurrency from environment
Missing LINKFLOW_API_KEY fails before any network client call when live mode is requested
Strict structured output schemas exist for scout maps, extractor claim attempts, verifier verdicts, and consistency checker findings
Tool loop uses explicit conversation history and function_call_output records
Malformed tool arguments fail loudly
Offline fake Linkflow responses support unit tests without credentials
Agent code cannot write directly to DuckDB
tests/test_linkflow_adapter.py and tests/test_tool_loop_offline.py pass
Existing linkflow_probe tests still pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Prove first offline vertical slice
userStories.description: As Claude reviewing Ralph output, I need one synthetic deal to move through atlas, one specialist, evidence binding, verifier, lifecycle aggregation, and review export without live credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator.run_synthetic_vertical_slice(run_dir, filing_path) exists
Synthetic fixture produces at least one confidentiality-agreement claim attempt
Claim quote is bound by deterministic verify_quote
Offline fake verifier is used when Linkflow env vars are absent
Exports include claim_cards.csv, review_queue.csv, human_decisions_template.csv, provider_calls.jsonl, and tool_calls.jsonl
Accepted claim has exact evidence
Rejected claim does not enter canonical eligibility
Partial correction creates a new attempt
All generated artifacts are under runs/<run_id>/<deal_slug>/
tests/test_orchestrator_vertical_slice.py and tests/test_exports.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Add deterministic canonical compiler
userStories.description: As the publication layer, I need accepted source-backed attempts to compile into canonical graph rows with row-evidence links.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimal canonical models exist for deal, filing, source_span, actor, event, event_actor_link, and canonical_row_evidence
Compiler reads accepted attempts, evidence bindings, coverage records, conflicts, and human decisions
Compiler refuses unbound attempts, missing evidence coordinates, failed_to_check coverage, and blocking conflicts
Canonical ids are deterministic from run id, deal slug, source ids, and payload keys
Deleting canonical rows and recompiling produces identical rows
Model-owned source offsets are impossible because compiler reads evidence bindings only
tests/test_canonical_compile.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Add human decision import and coverage ledger
userStories.description: As the hand-review interface, I need human decisions and coverage states represented in the data model without a UI dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: human_decisions_template.csv import supports accept, reject, correct, and defer
Human correct creates a new claim attempt unless the correction is notes-only
Coverage states include checked_found, checked_absent, ambiguous, not_applicable, and failed_to_check
failed_to_check blocks trusted canonical publication
checked_absent remains review-visible but does not invent a fact
tests/test_exports.py, tests/test_lifecycle.py, and tests/test_canonical_compile.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Add full agent role surface
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need the full V1 agent role surface without expanding canonical schema beyond proven vertical-slice needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Roles include scout, party_relation_extractor, timeline_bid_extractor, count_coverage_extractor, omission_inspector, verifier, and consistency_checker
Every role has a strict output schema
Every role has a prompt hash
No role exposes a comparison or answer-key tool
Omission inspector emits coverage checks only, not free-form speculation
No role writes truth directly
tests/test_agent_roles.py and tests/test_orchestrator_vertical_slice.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Run live synthetic Linkflow vertical slice
userStories.description: As the operator, I need one credential-backed synthetic run to prove the architecture with live Linkflow calls while keeping secrets out of artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI command python3 -m sec_review_compiler run-synthetic --run-root runs/dev --deal-slug synthetic-demo exists
CLI command python3 -m sec_review_compiler summarize-run runs/dev/<run_id>/synthetic-demo exists
Live mode without credentials fails before network
Live mode with credentials reads them from environment only and never prints them
Live synthetic run creates deal_room.duckdb, review exports, tool logs, provider logs, and canonical output
quality_reports/session_logs/2026-05-08_live_vertical_slice.md records redacted command, run directory, agent call count, claim count, verdict counts, canonical row count, and review queue count
python3 -m pytest -q passes
A targeted secret scan finds no real credential

userStories.title: Add verifier calibration seed
userStories.description: As Claude evaluating verifier rigor, I need a calibration artifact with adversarial cards and fail-closed metrics before trusting verifier output.
acceptanceCriteria: calibration/verifier_seed.jsonl contains at least 12 seed cards
Seed cards include confirmed-correct, single-field errors, multi-field errors, plausible hallucinations, ambiguous cases, and coverage gaps
Calibration runner sends cards through the verifier and computes category metrics
Calibration fails closed if all cards are rubber-stamped confirm
Wrong dates and wrong actors are not counted as confirmed-correct
tests/test_calibration.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Complete final acceptance audit
userStories.description: As Claude administering Ralph, I need final proof that the clean-slate implementation satisfies the specs, contains no stale authority surface, and leaks no secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -m pytest -q passes
python3 -m linkflow_probe summarize runs/linkflow-probe/20260508T123815Z prints Gate: GO
Source-tree stale-surface scan finds no live authority references to forbidden prior surfaces outside explicitly allowed archive/probe evidence
Secret scan finds no real credential
git ls-tree inventory contains no .env, .venv, __pycache__, .pytest_cache, .DS_Store, or raw provider secret file

quality_reports/session_logs/2026-05-08_agentic_review_compiler_acceptance.md records exact command outputs, probe run id, live vertical slice run id, known limitations, and next recommended extension
README.md exists and points to the specs, Ralph plan, ralph/prd.json, Linkflow probe evidence, and basic commands
US-001 through US-012 in ralph/prd.json are marked passes=true only after Claude verification notes are present; Claude marks US-013 after this final audit passes
